what_it_does: Provides geometric layout primitives for a circular wheel visualization and its inscribed square grid: constants, polar-to-cartesian placement, ring radii, sector coloring, directed chord paths, arrowheads, deterministic grid-cell jitter, dyadic nesting levels, and sector wedge SVG paths.
observations: Constants TAU, R_MIN, R_MAX, and WHEEL_FRAC are declared as fixed layout laws.
wheelGeom centers the wheel and sizes the inscribed circle to 35% of the smaller viewport dimension.
placePolar rotates theta by -PI/2 so angle 0 maps to the top (12 o'clock).
sectorHue derives HSL color from sector index and reads saturation/lightness from CSS custom properties.
chordPath draws quadratic Bezier chords between sector mid-angles, pulled toward the center.
arrowHead builds a triangle oriented along the vector from tail to tip.
gridCellCenter maps dyadic grid cells to square centers with a deterministic pseudo-random jitter via hash01.
dyadicLevels enumerates power-of-two subdivisions with decreasing opacity weights.
wedgePath emits an SVG sector wedge path from the center to radius R.
standouts: Heavy use of comments framing constants as 'LAW' and geometry as 'structural, not policed'.
Deterministic jitter using a sine hash instead of Math.random for stable sub-cell offsets.
Color tied to geometry through CSS variable-driven HSL so themes can re-tint live.
Directed chords rendered with source dot + arrowhead, and bidirectional cycles kept as cycles.
conventions: TypeScript module with exported utility functions and a few local helpers (cssVar, hash01).
Polar angles are pre-rotated by -90 degrees for a north-starting dial convention.
SVG path strings are built with template literals and explicit quadratic Bezier commands.
Naming mixes geometric shorthand (R, cx, cy) with longer descriptive names.
concerns: cssVar reads getComputedStyle on documentElement and will return fallback during SSR or non-browser environments; only checked via typeof window.
hash01 is a simple trig hash; collision quality and distribution are not validated for large grids.
chordPath's pull default of 0.5 may produce overlapping or ambiguous arcs for closely spaced sectors.
No input validation on radii, counts, or indices (e.g., rings <= 0, m <= 0).
notes: This is the shared geometry seed for the wheel/square visualization: every coordinate, color, and path primitive used by the surface app is meant to derive from these functions.
questions: What consumes these primitives — is there a single canvas/SVG renderer or multiple?
How are bidirectional edges represented in the input data (two directed entries or a flag)?
Are the CSS variables --pig-sat and --pig-lit defined in a global stylesheet or scoped per theme?
What is the intended behavior when width or height is zero or negative?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be the deterministic layout grid seed for the wheel and square; the file matches that purpose, supplying constants and pure geometry helpers without rendering or state management.
apparent_themes: Geometry as law: layout derived from a small set of canonical constants.
Determinism over randomness: stable, reproducible visual placement.
Separation of shape and pigment: color controlled by CSS variables, geometry by code.
Registry-truth: visual elements reflect supplied data rather than inventing relationships.
intention_signals: The file tries to encode the visual design system as a set of pure, reusable geometric rules so that the wheel, square, sectors, rings, and connections stay proportionally consistent and themable.
novelty: The combination of deterministic pseudo-random sub-cell jitter, CSS-variable-driven sector hue, and explicit 'north-start' polar rotation in a single layout-seed module.
contribution: A compact, deterministic geometry library that maps polar projection data to screen coordinates and produces SVG path primitives for sectors, rings, chords, and arrowheads, while keeping color themable via CSS variables.
summary_for_embedding: A TypeScript layout-seed module that defines constants and pure functions for placing polar projection points, drawing wheel rings, sectors, directed chords, arrowheads, and an inscribed square dyadic grid for a circular visualization.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single source of deterministic geometric primitives so the wheel-and-square visualization remains proportionally consistent and visually stable across renderers and themes.
suggested_fields: consumer_components: which UI components import these helpers
theme_contract: expected CSS custom properties and their defaults
coordinate_system_convention: documentation of angle origin and rotation
proposed_edge_kinds: theme_contract: CSS variables consumed by sectorHue
data_driven_render: functions expect indices/angles from engine

symbols.description: Defines an object type with x and y numeric coordinates, used throughout the module for points and vectors.

symbols.does: Computes wheel center and inscribed radius for a viewport.
symbols.description: Returns the center point (cx, cy) and radius R set to 35% of the smaller viewport dimension, ensuring the wheel stays centered and proportionally sized.

symbols.does: Converts polar coordinates to cartesian wheel coordinates.
symbols.description: Takes a normalized radius r and angle theta, rotates theta by -90 degrees so zero points north, and maps the point onto the wheel centered at (cx, cy) with radius R.

symbols.does: Computes pixel radii for concentric rings.
symbols.description: Given a ring count and wheel radius R, returns evenly spaced radii from R/rings up to R.

symbols.does: Returns an HSL color string for a sector index.
symbols.description: Maps sector index i out of n to a hue and reads saturation/lightness from CSS custom properties --pig-sat and --pig-lit, falling back to defaults.

symbols.does: Reads a CSS custom property with a fallback.
symbols.description: Local helper that returns the computed value of a CSS variable on the document root, or the provided fallback when running outside a browser.
Computes the midpoint angle between from and to, used as the attachment angle for directed chords.

symbols.does: Builds a quadratic Bezier chord path between two sector mid-angles.
symbols.description: Calculates from and to points on the wheel perimeter at the given end radius, then pulls the control point toward the center by pull to create an interior arc, returning the SVG path and geometry points.

symbols.does: Builds an SVG arrowhead triangle path.
symbols.description: Creates a small triangle at tip oriented along the direction from tail to tip, suitable for marking directed edges.
Local helper using a sine-based hash to produce stable fractional offsets from an integer seed.

symbols.does: Maps a dyadic grid cell to its center point with deterministic jitter.
symbols.description: Divides the inscribed square into an m by m grid, places the cell at its centre, and applies a bounded deterministic jitter so multiple points in the same cell remain visually separated.

symbols.does: Lists power-of-two subdivision levels for the square grid.
symbols.description: Enumerates divisions 2, 4, ..., m with decreasing opacity weights so coarser boundaries are drawn more strongly than finer ones.

symbols.does: Builds an SVG path for a sector wedge.
symbols.description: Returns a closed SVG path from the wheel center to the outer radius R spanning the angle range [from, to], using the large-arc flag when the span exceeds PI.
